  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Copper tubing was not reamed after cutting, leaving a visible internal burr."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out the affected joint, ream the tubing to its full inside diameter to remove all burrs, and re-solder or re-connect using a new fitting."
  ],
  "rationale": "Both the International Plumbing Code (IPC Section 605.14.1) and manufacturer specifications require all cut copper tubing to be reamed to its full inside diameter. Leaving an internal burr restricts water flow, causes localized turbulence, and can lead to premature erosion-corrosion and pinhole leaks in the copper piping system.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 605.14.1",
    "Copper Development Association (CDA) Copper Tube Handbook"
  ]
}
